level_signals: 3-5 years of experience building and leading acquisition and growth initiatives
Base pay range $136,000–$160,000 USD (Zone 1), consistent with mid-senior IC
Expected to communicate with senior leadership and drive strategy independently

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 3-5 years of experience building and leading acquisition and growth initiatives for credit card or other lending products.
Hands-on experience with SQL, R, or Python (specifically Pandas, Scikit-learn, etc.).
Strong communication skills and the ability to simplify complex programs to focus on key areas of improvement.
Experience with experimentation, including test design, reading results, and communicating insights to guide decisions.
Demonstrated ability to collaborate with partners across Product, Engineering, Data Science, Legal, and Compliance.
Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Information Systems, Analytics, Mathematics, Physics, Applied Sciences, or a related field.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience growing and scaling credit card programs is a plus.

scope.surface: Credit card growth strategy — acquisition campaigns, NPV modeling, marketing performance
scope.business_model: consumer fintech, B2C credit card product
primary_metrics_inferred: Net Present Value (NPV) of customer mix
core_responsibilities: Use data, technology, and customer-focused thinking to design initiatives that expand our credit card business.
Collaborate with partners across product, operations, and engineering to build and run campaigns that drive high response rates for the credit card product.
Use Net Present Value (NPV) models to design a growth strategy that delivers a resilient mix of customers to the card program.
Propose novel solutions and gather data independently to solve complex problems.
Oversee, build, and monitor marketing campaigns, using feedback loops to continuously improve performance.
Communicate clearly and effectively with senior leadership, product managers, and engineering partners, tailoring content to the audience and ensuring alignment.

compensation.equity_form: equity ownership mentioned as part of total rewards

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed across three geographic zones
Clearly scoped role with defined ownership of credit card growth strategy
Specific tooling requirements (SQL, Python, R) signal technical rigor rather than vague 'data skills'
Equity, bonus, and 401(k) matching mentioned in addition to base pay
Benefits package is comprehensive (100% paid health insurance for employees, 90% for dependents)
red_flags: Hybrid requirement of at least 3 days per week in Menlo Park CA, New York NY, or Washington DC — eliminates fully remote candidates
Visa sponsorship status not addressed anywhere in the JD
Team size and reporting structure not disclosed
3-5 year experience range specifically tied to credit card or lending products — narrow domain requirement

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years range + comp zone)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
compensation.base_range_usd uses Zone 1 (highest zone) as the stated range per schema convention; Zone 2 and Zone 3 ranges also exist in JD
ambiguities: The JD lists Washington, DC in the body as a valid office location, and Zone 1 compensation includes Menlo Park CA, New York NY, Bellevue WA, and Washington DC — Bellevue WA is not named as an office location in the role description, only in the pay table.
Visa sponsorship is neither confirmed nor denied; treated as 'unclear'.

The role title includes 'Analyst' but the scope and expectations (owning growth strategy, communicating with senior leadership, NPV modeling) are closer to a senior IC than a traditional analyst — seniority flagged as mid-senior IC.
